transcript.text: Okay travel with Kyle Mexico so I cross into Mexico from Brownsville Texas and
I go into some town I can't remember the name of it which is right on the eastern
Coast and I saw the Rio Grande River and it was a swamp you could have walked
Across it and I go and there's this like pedestrian mall there and I get a hotel
Room and I think at first I went to the Mac to the bank I didn't speak any
Spanish at all I took like a semester or something with Spanish like us when I was
Like a freshman in college but I didn't know any Spanish I took some when I was in
Seventh grade but pretty much did not and the lady didn't even speak English at
This bank but she did say cross the street say where I could get the money
From a money machine and then I saw some kid he was talking to said a couple of
Words I thought he was fluent English and I started talking and I realized I didn't
Know he was speaking English I don't recall Mexicans really speaking English but I
Got a room on this mall and then I realized that I didn't have some stuff I
Needed for bikes so I rode back into Brownsville and to the bike shop which
Is interesting that I can go back into America so easily for me in Mexico like
I could have been living really cheaply in Mexico and then working in America
And just coming across so again so then I went back into Mexico again and then I
Rode down south and it was like crossing into Mexico it was like total culture
Shock totally different from America and as I went farther south you just tell you
Know it's in another country roads were a lot more crowded you know older trucks
And cars and you know like outside cafes and stuff like that so I did have some
Weed that this kid named Marky gave me this guy named Marky gave me that I brought
Across um so I pulled on the side of the road and I smoked some and then uh ride and uh and
It wasn't as hot in Mexico as it was in Texas like I was okay to ride throughout the day and I
Think maybe because I was right next to the ocean even though I couldn't see the ocean the whole time
But uh and so I stayed at hotels and went down I think it ended up taking me something like 11
Days to get from like Austin to Mexico City so I'm like riding like maybe 100 110 or something like
That miles a day and um and there were some parts where the roads weren't that crowded once I was out in
The booties but um there were lots of outdoor like burrito stands and burritos were good they were
Different from in America they're good good enough and uh I stopped in this place I can't remember
What it's called but uh I just stayed in like Tampico which was interesting um and then rode and stayed
And then checked out a I stayed in one town uh just north of El Tajin or El Tajin in English which is
One of the like the more northernmost Mayan runs and it was really interesting because it's like
Really really packed like lots of pyramids not so big but they're totally jam-packed together um
Another beautiful place but I looked at the stones these granite stones and they were cut perfectly
Like you cut them with a laser and um so I don't know how those Mayans cut it but I mean use some kind
Of special uh technology and I didn't even know like Mayans back then had like iron tools but
I didn't even know if an iron tool could have done this this is like perfectly cut granite stone
But um oh yeah and then at the mouth I saw the Mexicans like I guess the Mayans had some
Traditional thing where they would hang by their feet from the top of some tower and then they would
Like unwind as they flew down so anyway so I spent two nights in that town then I rode up
Towards Mexico City so it was basically straight up and it was really fun because I was kind of
Getting into shape and I was I was on a mountain bike um not carrying too much weight just on the
Backside and uh so I was hammering up this hill hardcore like hammering strong and uh
I uh and tons of switchbacks and I could you know see how all the uh all the uh vegetation was
Changing like from jungle to more temperate climate and I stopped at one restaurant and they took like
An hour to get me fish and totally wasted my time pissed me off but I finally got it and uh and then I
Got on the road and there was but I was going up it's just just like almost as fast as these old
Trucks they have so I grabbed under the back of one and it pulled me all the way to the top
And then I think there was another one that passed me that I was drafting off of
And I think they were even helping me because when they pulled off they waved to me and then I rode down
Into some town it was like 60 miles north of Mexico City I can't remember what it's called but it was in the
Kind of the northern part of the valley and I could see how they were constructing some super highway it
Was like some big deep crevice that was kind of going off so maybe it's done by now I don't know
It goes all the way to the coast but I stayed there and then the next day I rode from that town
Into Mexico City and uh but I stayed like two hours in Teotihuacan checking out all those pyramids
Pyramid of sun on the moon I guess the pyramid of the sun is like the third biggest pyramid in the
World um after the great pyramid and even bigger than the second biggest pyramid in Egypt and the
Biggest pyramid was actually in Pueblo Mexico which is actually kind of like grown over but uh
It was cool it was like the perfect setting for a movie I think maybe maybe Beastmaster was filmed
There but just seeing that and um it's heyday it would have been beautiful it was a huge complex lots of
Other little mini pyramids and um I think it was in the northern end of uh some big lake that was
There when the Spaniards first got there but once Teotihuacan is pretty much the northern end of Mexico
City because I had to pee and when I left I couldn't find a single place to pee because it was just packed
With people I think I eventually had to go into a place or something and I went in and I was like a
Northern part where there is this big hill just north of the center part of Mexico City so you
Could go around this way around this way and I asked all these people what's the best way to get
There and they kept saying this way but I went down really far and I kind of felt like I wasn't
Going into Mexico City I thought they were lying so I started to turn around but it was raining
Really heavily so heavily that none of the cars could drive on the road there was a river about
Two feet on the road and I had to even I could like barely ride my bike through it and the only
Cars that could drive it were these trucks these big buses and so I grabbed on the back of them and
They'd pull me through and then little kids were playing in it and I could um and it could actually
Sweep some people away it was like it was a river in the road and so I was riding through this I went
Around the other end of the mountain and riding through the uh the water and it was kind of getting
Dark when I finally got into the center of Mexico City where there's that huge uh plaza it's the
I think it's like the biggest plaza I've ever seen it's huge and I got a hotel room and I was really
Sick I think I was sick I was there for like four days because I was just really sick with a fever
Maybe because I was just riding my bike too hard sometimes I get sick when I ride too much
Happened to me in Morocco because of that so uh I was there but I saw all the sights I Mexico City is
Beautiful one of the most beautiful cities I've ever seen because they have from the central square
To the I guess top of a hill north of it where the uh king or something whatever whatever you call
The magistrate lived you know there's this big palace that I saw and it goes and there's this big
Avenue like uh like the Champs Elysees that just goes directly from the plaza to this hill and the hill
Was in this big giant park that had really wide roads that was closed off to cars that I rode my bike
Around and they had um lots of different cool stuff going on there um I got some food and
Um and I saw all the museums and there were lots of uh Aztec pyramids right next to the plaza
When the Spaniards came and took over the Aztecs they built their uh their churches right on top of
The pyramids and so they had this one church painted in red right on the south side of the plaza
But it was because they built it on a lake it was wet and the church was totally on its side
Like this you could see that building laying on its side and some other buildings also
And then just next to that they had excavated the Aztec pyramids and you could see the side of it
Up like this and uh it was cool and inside was a really good um museum of the Aztec
Empire oh I guess that Mexico would have another part
